# Transcrição: Playing_in_the_middle

- **Fonte:** Playing_in_the_middle
- **Duração:** 01:43
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:43]

I've been watching a series on Netflix recently. Have you seen the squid game? Have you heard of the squid game? This won't spoil the thing for you, but right at the end, there's an old man that right at the end, he's like, spoil the thing for you. I'm just going to share the ending. I will, but I promise it won't because you won't have context until you watch it. Right. In the end, the old man says something that I thought was really fascinating. He goes, do you know what someone who has lots of money has in common with someone who has no money? Living is not fun for either of them. It's boring. And I thought, wow, that's really interesting. When we see those who have millions of dollars and we see them on Instagram or we see them on social media, to us, it seems so exciting because they've just bought a new Bentley. They've just bought a mansion. They've just bought a jet. But the reality behind this is that those people who have those things and have the capacity to buy those things, they can buy 20 Bentley's if they wanted to. They could buy 50. So to them, it's boring. And what you learn through watching this series is that, oh, those who are ridiculously wealthy, they're bored. And those who don't have enough resources are kind of bored and suffering as well. So they're both at each end of the spectrum suffering in their own right. And it kind of reveals to us that the best place to live is well, kind of in the middle. Because in the middle, you're not quite suffering as if you have no financial abundance, but then you're not quite suffering on the other end. The middle is kind of the way, right? I think this is why people yo-yo between being unhappy and happy. When I focus too much on work, I'm unhappy. Probably because I'm doing too much work and it's becoming boring to me. And then when I focus too much on my personal life, and again, like you said, being with family, following my certain interests, then I also get unhappy. It's that middle ground, isn't it? With everything, it's that middle ground.
